definition:
1. like Lat. summus for supremus, the highest, uppermost , of Zeus, ὕπατος κρειόντων, θεῶν ὕπ., (Homer); οἱ ὕπατοι the gods above , Lat. superi, opposite to οἱ χθόνιοι, Lat. inferi, (Aeschulus Tragicus); ὕπατός τις some god above , (Aeschulus Tragicus) 
2. simply of Place, ἐν πυρῇ ὑπάτῃ on the very top  of the funeral pile, (Iliad by Homer) 
3. of Time, last , Lat. supremus, (Sophocles Tragicus) 
5. with genitive, like the preposition ὑπό, ὕπατος χώρας supreme over  the land, (Aeschulus Tragicus); ὕπατοι λεχέων high above  the nest, (Aeschulus Tragicus) 
6. as Substantive, ὕπατος, the Roman consul , (Polybius Historicus), etc. (ML)
